title: Exam 1 posted — due Friday 5/8 by 11:59 PM
body:
Exam 1 is up. Download "Modeling Problems" from the Resources page (or the Lessons page under Week 7).

Due: Friday, May 8 at 11:59 PM.

Three modeling problems in one workbook:
1) Bollinger Bands — build a 20-day moving average ±1σ and ±2σ on Tesla daily prices, then chart it.
2) Sector Allocation — work through the S&P 500 list and decide how to allocate 100 portfolio positions across the 11 GICS sectors based on cost, time, and rigor.
3) Pro Forma — build a simplified P&G income statement for FY26 using the historical data and the scenario probabilities.

Each problem has its own instructions tab with step-by-step guidance and the questions you need to answer.

Open notes, open everything. You can use any tool, any AI, any source. The goal is to produce a working model — show your work in the spreadsheet itself (formulas, not pasted values).

If anything in the workbook looks broken or unclear, post in the discussion or email me — don't guess.

title: Welcome — start with probability
body:
Welcome, class. Take a look around the site and let me know if anything looks off or you have comments.

Go ahead and start working through the probability lecture up on the Lessons page — that's Week 4, Chapter 5. I'll drop some supplemental exercises alongside it shortly.

Stay tuned.
